name: Pont-to-point Joint
description: A physics constraint that connects two objects at specific local pivot points, allowing rotation in all directions while keeping the pivot points coincident. Also known as a ball-and-socket joint, it permits 3 rotational degrees of freedom but locks translational movement at the connection points. Useful for creating freely rotating connections like shoulder joints or chain links. KEY FEATURES:
documentation:
## Point-to-Point Joint
Connect two objects with a flexible ball-and-socket constraint.

### Settings
- **Other Object** - The object to connect
- **Collision Enabled** - Allow collisions between joined objects
- **Pivot A** - Connection point on this object (X, Y, Z)
- **Pivot B** - Connection point on the other object (X, Y, Z)

### Tips
- Objects can rotate freely around the pivot points
- Use for chains, ropes, ragdolls, and wrecking balls
- Both objects need physics enabled
author: stemstudio

objectB.name: Other object
objectB.description: The second physics-enabled object to connect with this ball-and-socket joint.

collisionEnabled.name: Collision enabled
collisionEnabled.description: When enabled, the two joined objects can still collide with each other. Disable to allow them to overlap.

pivotA.name: Pivot A
pivotA.description: The local-space connection point on this object where the joint attaches.

x.name: X
x.description: X offset of the pivot point in this object's local space.

y.name: Y
y.description: Y offset of the pivot point in this object's local space.

z.name: Z
z.description: Z offset of the pivot point in this object's local space.

pivotB.name: Pivot B
pivotB.description: The local-space connection point on the other object where the joint attaches.

x.name: X
x.description: X offset of the pivot point in the other object's local space.

y.name: Y
y.description: Y offset of the pivot point in the other object's local space.

z.name: Z
z.description: Z offset of the pivot point in the other object's local space.
